text.title: MongoDB Document Design
text.summary: Embedding vs referencing decisions, bucket pattern for time-series, and indexing rules.

rule.statement: Embed child documents when they are always accessed with the parent and the child set is bounded (1:few); reference when the relationship is 1:many or many:many.
rule.rationale: Embedding avoids joins and keeps reads in one round-trip; unbounded embedded arrays grow the document past the 16MB limit and degrade index performance.

rule.statement: Use the bucket pattern for time-series data: group N measurements per document with a summary sub-document; set a TTL index for automatic expiration.
rule.rationale: One document per measurement creates millions of tiny documents, degrading index performance. Buckets reduce document count by 100-1000x.

rule.statement: Create a compound index for every common (field1, sort_field) query; add partial indexes for frequently queried status subsets; set TTL indexes on expiring data.
rule.rationale: Missing indexes on query fields cause collection scans; partial indexes avoid indexing documents outside the filter, keeping index size small.

rule.statement: Enable $jsonSchema validation on every production collection; never deploy a collection without validation.
rule.rationale: Schemaless collections accumulate orphan fields and inconsistent types silently; validation catches bad writes at the DB layer.
